conditions.commentary: CERN-OHL-P is the permissive variant; unlike CERN-OHL-W (weakly reciprocal) and CERN-OHL-S (strongly reciprocal), the permissive variant imposes no obligation to make Source available when Conveying modified Covered Source or Products. Section 3.4 explicitly allows Conveying under different licence terms.
Network/remote-use disclosure is not a natural concept for physical hardware products and is not addressed by CERN-OHL-P.

Section 3.4 explicitly allows Conveying Covered Source or modified Covered Source under licence terms which differ from CERN-OHL-P, so long as Notices are retained and a copy of this Licence is provided. No same-license requirement for downstream.

limitations.commentary: No affirmative trademark grant; section 7.2 imposes a narrow restriction on use of the Licensor's or CERN's name/image/logo to prevent suggestion of endorsement, but this is a name/endorsement clause rather than a general denial of trademark rights.
